item.title: 从源代码构建一个最小的 Linux 操作系统🏗 | Building a minimal Linux OS from source code 🏗
item.description:
在现代世界中，我们被大量复杂程度不同的电子设备所包围。 如果设备或多或少复杂，例如电视、路由器或智能手机，那么它运行 Linux 操作系统的可能性很大，这个想法困扰着我。

更让我困扰的是，在各种设备和服务器上运行的 Linux 操作系统的所有内核都是从位于kernel.org网站上的存储库中的源代码构建的。

如此不同的设备，以及在它们上面运行的操作系统，都是由同一个源代码组装而成的！ 当然，这种说法只是部分正确，因为实际上内核通常是由特定 Linux 发行版的开发人员以及特定设备的开发人员扩展和修改的，但是有很多共同的源代码。

一直想自己从源代码构建Linux操作系统，但过程总是显得扑朔迷离，有很多不懂的地方。 但是，在某个时间点，我仍然积累了足够的知识来实现​​我的梦想。 在这篇文章中，我将向您展示如何在您的计算机上从源代码编译和运行最小化 Linux。

它不会让您使用计算机的所有功能，但它具有主要功能 — 命令行界面。 相信我，在您的真实计算机上获得一个可用的 Linux 命令行界面将是一种令人难以置信的体验。

令人惊讶的是，获取 Linux 命令行所需的最小集合仅包含两个文件：Linux 内核文件和根文件系统初始映像文件。 显然，需要一个引导加载程序来加载这两个文件并启动内核的执行，并在必要时向其传递初始根文件系统的映像和其他参数。

最小的 Linux 操作系统

为了让您能够以某种方式使用操作系统，您需要四个组件：

引导加载程序：一种特殊程序，允许处理器开始执行位于操作系统内核文件中的机器指令；

内核：包含以下内容的程序代码：

  • 处理器可以使用的各种物理 I/O 设备的抽象（设备驱动程序），
  • 存储数据结构的抽象（文件系统），
  • 程序指令（进程、线程）时间分离的抽象，
  • 其他抽象。

由于有了内核，应用程序的开发者往往不关心计算机上安装了哪种显卡、键盘或硬盘。 他或她只是编写与 I/O 设备、进程、文件、套接字等一起工作的代码。

初始根文件系统：需要内核才能执行进一步加载操作系统所需的文件的初始加载。 最重要的是未包含在内核文件中但需要进一步加载的 Linux 内核模块，以及操作系统启动时第一个进程将基于该文件创建的文件 ( init )。

一组作为操作系统内核接口的实用程序：允许您使用操作系统内核中的抽象。 根据运行操作系统的设备的复杂性和用途，此集合可能会有所不同。 实用程序定义功能和用户界面。 例如，路由器将有一个； 一个电话会有另一个； 个人计算机将有三分之一。

加载Linux操作系统

计算机打开。

  • 操作系统引导加载程序将 Linux 内核文件和初始文件系统映像文件（ initrd文件）加载到 RAM 中。
  • 操作系统引导加载程序将控制权转移到 Linux 操作系统内核。
  • 操作系统内核执行初始化。
  • 操作系统内核访问初始文件系统映像（挂载映像）中的文件。
  • 内核在初始文件系统中寻找一个init文件，并根据它启动第一个用户进程。
  • 发行版是一个 Linux 内核和一组安装在计算机或设备上的库、实用程序和程序。

    目前，不同发行版的数量是巨大的。 您可以在DistroWatch上查看它们的列表。

    现代 Linux 发行版通常以 ISO 映像的形式分发，并允许您安装更新和附加程序（软件包），但我们正在制作最小发行版，因此我们不会有这样的机会。

    有关如何从头开始构建 Linux 发行版的最完整说明之一位于此处。 构建 Linux 发行版是一个有趣的过程，可以让你学到很多新东西，但它非常耗时，这意味着你需要很大的意志力才能从头到尾执行它。

    我试图将分发工具包的创建简化到最低限度：我们不会挂载永久文件系统，而是作为一个init文件，我们将使用一个脚本文件来执行最少的初始化并启动sh shell。

    操作系统引导加载

    在它存在的这些年里，Linux 已经被移植到许多硬件平台上。 每个平台的 Linux 引导是不同的。 对于 x86，下载可能有所不同，如下所示：

    1. 它将用于引导 BIOS 或 UEFI 吗？
    2. 如何标记硬盘或闪存驱动器（MBR 或 GPT）？
    3. 内核文件和带有初始根文件系统映像的文件（称为initrd ）位于什么介质和什么文件系统（FAT、NTFS、EXT、CDFS 等）中？

    初始根文件系统的结构

    初始根文件系统包含进一步 Linux 操作所需的最少数量的文件和目录。 在我们的例子中，这些是bin 、 dev 、 proc和sys目录。 实用工具包

    内核和命令行实用程序由不同的程序员团队开发。

    最常见的集合是：

    由于其简单性和低磁盘空间，BusyBox 通常用于嵌入式设备。 为了简单起见，我将使用它。

    创建 Linux 构建环境

    听起来很矛盾，Linux 通常被编译成 Linux。 为此，您需要一个 Linux 操作系统，其中包含允许您构建 Linux 内核的程序和一组用于构建的实用程序。

    例如，在 Ubuntu 22.10 上，我们需要安装以下软件包： make 、 build-essential 、 bc 、 bison 、 flex 、 libssl-dev 、 libelf-dev 、 wget 、 cpio 、 fdisk 、 extlinux 、 dosfstools和qemu-system-x86 。

    请注意，对于其他系统，软件包集可能会有所不同。

    在 Ubuntu 22.10 上构建最小 Linux

    • 安装程序集所需的包。
     $ cd ~ $ mkdir -p simple-linux/build/sources $ mkdir -p simple-linux/build/downloads $ mkdir -p simple-linux/build/out $ mkdir -p simple-linux/linux $ sudo apt update $ sudo apt install --yes make build-essential bc bison flex libssl-dev libelf-dev wget cpio fdisk extlinux dosfstools qemu-system-x86
    • 从 Internet 下载 Linux 内核和 BusyBox 的源代码。
     $ cd simple-linux/build $ wget -P downloads https://cdn.kernel.org/pub/linux/kernel/v5.x/linux-5.15.79.tar.xz $ wget -P downloads https://busybox.net/downloads/busybox-1.35.0.tar.bz2
    • 用源代码解压档案。
     $ tar -xvf downloads/linux-5.15.79.tar.xz -C sources $ tar -xjvf downloads/busybox-1.35.0.tar.bz2 -C sources
    • 也为 Linux 内核构建 BusyBox 二进制文件。 此过程需要一些时间，大约 10 分钟甚至更长时间，因此请不要惊慌。
     $ cd sources/busybox-1.35.0 $ make defconfig $ make LDFLAGS=-static $ cp busybox ../../out/ $ cd ../linux-5.15.79 $ make defconfig $ make -j8 || exit $ cp arch/x86_64/boot/bzImage ~/simple-linux/linux/vmlinuz-5.15.79
     $ mkdir -p ~/simple-linux/build/initrd $ cd ~/simple-linux/build/initrd $ vi init

    除了vim编辑器（ vi命令），您还可以使用其他文本编辑器，例如gedit 。

    创建目录和文件的结构。

    •  $ chmod 777 init $ mkdir -p bin dev proc sys $ cd bin $ cp ~/simple-linux/build/out/busybox ./ $ for prog in $(./busybox --list); do ln -s /bin/busybox $prog; done
    • 将结构放在initrd文件中，这是我们的cpio存档。
     $ cd .. $ find . | cpio -o -H newc > ~/simple-linux/linux/initrd-busybox-1.35.0.img
    • 在 QEMU 模拟器中启动构建的映像。
     $ cd ~/simple-linux/linux $ qemu-system-x86_64 -kernel vmlinuz-5.15.79 -initrd initrd-busybox-1.35.0.img -nographic -append 'console=ttyS0'
    • 让我们尝试输入您知道的 Linux 命令。 通过键入 exit 命令退出模拟的 Linux。

    为闪存驱动器创建引导映像

    如果我们想在真实硬件上运行我们的 Linux，那么最简单的方法可能是创建一个映像以放在引导驱动器上并刻录它。 在我看来，创建这样的映像并从驱动器启动是最困难的过程，需要您掌握更多高级知识。

    创建图像时，需要做出几个决定：

    • 什么将启动引导（BIOS 或 UEFI）？
    • 您将使用哪种驱动器（CDROM、闪存驱动器或硬盘驱动器）？
    • 您将如何对驱动器进行分区（MBR、GPT），您是否会对它进行分区？
    • 您将使用哪个引导加载程序？
    • 将使用什么文件系统以及 Linux 和引导加载程序文件位于何处？

    我使用了一个安装了 MBR 和 EXTLINUX 引导加载程序的闪存驱动器以及文件所在的一个 FAT32 分区。 创建可引导闪存驱动器映像的算法如下：

     $ dd if=/dev/zero of=boot-disk.img bs=1024K count=50
    • 在映像文件中创建引导分区。
     $ echo "type=83,bootable" | sfdisk boot-disk.img
    • 在boot-disk.img文件内的引导分区上设置环回设备。
     $ losetup -D $ LOOP_DEVICE=$(losetup -f) $ losetup -o $(expr 512 \* 2048) ${LOOP_DEVICE} boot-disk.img
    • 在环回设备上创建一个文件系统。
     $ mkfs.vfat ${LOOP_DEVICE}
    • 挂载环回设备。
     $ mkdir -p /mnt/os $ mount -t auto ${LOOP_DEVICE} /mnt/os
    • 将 Linux 内核文件和initrd文件复制到boot-disk.img文件内的第一个分区。
     $ cp vmlinuz-5.15.79 initrd-busybox-1.35.0.img /mnt/os
    • 在boot-disk.img文件中，放置 EXTLINUX 引导加载程序。
     $ mkdir -p /mnt/os/boot $ extlinux --install /mnt/os/boot
    • 为引导加载程序创建一个配置文件，我们在其中指明要加载的内容。
     $ echo "DEFAULT linux" >> /mnt/os/boot/syslinux.cfg $ echo " SAY Booting Simple Linux via SYSLINUX" >> /mnt/os/boot/syslinux.cfg $ echo " LABEL linux" >> /mnt/os/boot/syslinux.cfg $ echo " KERNEL /vmlinuz-5.15.79" >> /mnt/os/boot/syslinux.cfg $ echo " APPEND initrd=/initrd-busybox-1.35.0.img nomodeset" >> /mnt/os/boot/syslinux.cfg
    • 卸载环回设备。
     $ umount /mnt/os $ losetup -D
    • 在boot-disk.img文件内的磁盘开头安装 MBR 引导加载程序。
     $ dd if=/usr/lib/syslinux/mbr/mbr.bin of=boot-disk.img bs=440 count=1 conv=notrunc

    使用 Docker 构建 Linux

    上述算法包含许多命令和参数； 打字时很容易出错。 命令可以组合成 bash 脚本，为了能够在 Windows 10 或 11 操作系统上构建 Linux，使用 Docker Desktop 是合理的。

    在 Dockerfile 中，您描述程序或脚本的环境结构。

  • 使用基于 Dockerfile 的 Docker 实用程序，您可以以特定格式创建此环境的映像。
  • 使用相同的实用程序，您可以启动在隔离环境中运行的基于图像的程序或脚本实例，在 Docker 术语中称为Docker 容器。
  • 您创建的图像可以存储在存储库中并重复使用。 从同一图像创建的 Docker 容器将在所有可以运行它们的计算机上以相同的方式运行。
  • 以下是 Dockerfile 的内容：

     FROM ubuntu:22.10 RUN apt update && apt install --yes make build-essential bc bison flex libssl-dev libelf-dev wget RUN apt install --yes cpio fdisk extlinux dosfstools qemu-system-x86 RUN apt install --yes vim ARG APP=/app ARG LINUX_DIR=$APP/linux ARG FILES_DIR=$APP/files ARG SCRIPTS_DIR=$APP/scripts ENV BUILD_DIR=$APP/build ENV LINUX_DIR=$LINUX_DIR ENV FILES_DIR=$FILES_DIR ENV LINUX_VER=5.15.79 ENV BUSYBOX_VER=1.35.0 ENV BASH_ENV="$SCRIPTS_DIR/bash-env/env" COPY ./scripts $APP/scripts COPY ./files $APP/files RUN mkdir -p $LINUX_DIR RUN ln -s $APP/scripts/start-linux.sh /usr/bin/start &&\ ln -s $APP/scripts/build-linux.sh /usr/bin/build &&\ ln -s $APP/scripts/build-image.sh /usr/bin/image WORKDIR $APP/scripts CMD build

    让我们快速浏览一下 Dockerfile 的主要命令：

    它指定我们的 Linux 构建映像将基于哪个文件系统映像。 在本例中，它是 Ubuntu 22.10。

    也就是说，如果您正在运行 Ubuntu 22.10 并在命令行上执行它们，那么 RUN 之后的命令将被执行。 作为该命令的结果，您的文件系统映像将发生变化，因为这些命令会更改其中的文件系统。

    与 RUN 一样，它修改映像中的文件系统。

    不知道说清楚了没有， ARG是创建镜像时使用的变量， ENV是基于这个创建已经创建容器时使用的变量图像，并且这些变量将在其中可见。

    使用 Docker 运行和构建最小的 Linux

    你可以试验我的项目。 在 Windows 上，最好在 PowerShell 中运行 Docker。

     $ git clone https://github.com/artyomsoft/simple-linux.git $ cd simple-linux $ docker build --build-arg APP -t simple-linux .
     $ mkdir linux $ cd linux $ docker run -v ${pwd}:/app/linux --rm -it simple-linux build

    您创建的 linux 目录将包含构建的 Linux 内核文件和初始根系统映像文件。

    • 为闪存驱动器创建引导映像。

    请注意，您需要在 Docker 中使用--privileged选项，因为图像脚本使用环回设备。

     $ docker run -v ${pwd}:/app/linux –-privileged --rm -it simple-linux image

    如果您使用适用于 Linux 的 Docker Desktop，则需要使用sudo运行 Docker，并且需要使用${pwd}而不是$(pwd) 。

    将闪存驱动器的引导映像刻录到媒体

    可以使用Win32DiskImager实用程序将为闪存驱动器创建的映像文件 ( linux-5.15.79-busybox-1.35.0-disk.img ) 写入闪存驱动器。

    需要注意的是，写入时会丢失U盘上所有的数据，所以最好使用没有任何文件的U盘！

    将映像写入闪存驱动器后，重新启动计算机并选择从 USB-HDD 启动，即从您创建的闪存驱动器。 很可能，在此之前，您需要在 BIOS 中选择“Legacy Boot”并禁用“Secure Boot”。

    结论

    在这篇博文中，我提供了有关如何从源代码获得可运行的 Linux 系统的详细说明。

    对某些人来说，这篇文章可能看起来很简单，不值得关注。 但是，为了不被细节吓跑，我没有深入探讨 BIOS、UEFI、文件系统、引导加载程序、 glibc库、详细的操作系统引导过程、各种规范、动态和静态链接、Linux 内核等主题模块，... 我只是给出了最少量的理论，可以让你理解实际上你做了什么，并且比我更快地理解这个主题，而不需要一点一点地收集所有信息。

    您以后几乎不会使用由此产生的操作系统，但我希望您对 Linux 的抽象知识能转化为您的理解和技能。

    这篇文章有帮助吗？ 您是否尝试过从源代码构建一个最小的 Linux 操作系统？ 在评论中分享您的经验！

    顺便说一句，你可以给我买杯咖啡来支持我的工作！ 我会在这里为你留下几个链接:)

    你也可以在Coinbase上支持我



    • 原文:

    In the modern world, we are surrounded by a huge number of electronic devices of varying degrees of complexity. If the device is more or less complex, for example, a TV, router, or smartphone, then with a high degree of probability it is running the Linux operating system, and this thought haunts me.

    What haunts me even more is the fact that all the kernels of the Linux operating system that run on various devices and servers are built from the source code located in the repository on the kernel.org website.

    Such different devices, and the operating system running on them is assembled from the same source code! This statement is, of course, only partly true, since in fact the kernel is usually extended and modified by the developers of specific Linux distributions as well as the developers of specific devices, but there is a lot of common source code.

    I've always wanted to build the Linux operating system myself from source code, but the process has always seemed complicated and confusing, and there's a lot I don't understand. But still, at a certain point in time, I accumulated enough knowledge to fulfill my dream. In this post, I'll show you how to compile and run minimal Linux from source on your computer.

    It will not allow you to use all the features of your computer, but it will have the main thing — a command-line interface. Trust me, getting a working Linux command-line interface on your real computer will be an incredible experience.

    Surprisingly, the minimum set required to obtain the Linux command line only consists of two files: the Linux kernel file and the root-filesystem initial image file. Obviously, a bootloader is needed that will load these two files and initiate the execution of the kernel, with passing it the image of the initial root filesystem and other parameters, if necessary.

    Minimal Linux OS

    In order for you to somehow be able to work with the operating system, you need four components:

    Bootloader: a special program that allows the processor to start executing machine instructions located in the kernel file of the OS;

    Kernel: the program code that contains: 

    • abstractions for the various physical I/O devices that the processor can work with (device drivers), 
    • abstractions of data structures for storage (file systems),
    •  abstractions for the time separation of program instructions (processes, threads), 
    • other abstractions.

    Because of the kernel, the developer of application programs often doesn't care which video card, keyboard, or hard drive is installed on the computer. He or she simply writes code that works with I/O devices, processes, files, sockets, etc.

    Initial root filesystem: is needed in order for the kernel to perform the initial loading of files necessary for further loading of the OS. The most important are the Linux kernel modules that are not included in the kernel file but are needed for further loading and the file on the basis of which the very first process will be created when the OS boots (init).

    Set of utilities that is an interface to the OS kernel: allows you to work with abstractions found in the kernel of an OS. Depending on the complexity and purpose of the device on which the operating system will run, this set may vary. Utilities define the functionality and user interface. For example, a router will have one; a phone will have another; and a personal computer will have a third. 

    Loading the Linux operating system

    The boot of the Linux operating system may differ on different architectures and computers, but for the x86 architecture, the boot looks like this (in most cases):

    1. The computer turns on.
    2. The BIOS or UEFI finds the OS bootloader on the computer and transfers control to it.
    3. The OS bootloader loads the Linux kernel file and the initial filesystem image file (the initrd file) into RAM.
    4. The OS bootloader transfers control to the Linux OS kernel.
    5. The OS kernel performs initialization.
    6. The OS kernel accesses the files that are in the initial filesystem image (mounts the image).
    7. The kernel looks for an init file in the initial filesystem and starts the very first user process based on it.
    8. The init process mounts an already persistent filesystem, continues to initialize the OS, transfers the root of the Linux filesystem to the mounted filesystem, and starts other processes that are needed for initialization.

    Linux distributions

    A distribution is a Linux kernel and a set of libraries, utilities, and programs that are installed on a computer or device.

    At the moment, the number of different distributions is huge. You can view a list of them on the DistroWatch.

    Modern Linux distributions are usually distributed in the form of ISO images and allow you to install updates and additional programs (packages), but we are making a minimal distribution, so we will not have such an opportunity.

    One of the most complete instructions on how to build a Linux distribution from scratch is here. Building a Linux distribution is an interesting process that will allow you to learn a lot of new things, but it is very time-consuming, which means you need a lot of willpower to carry it out from start to finish.

    I tried to simplify the creation of the distribution kit to a minimum: we will not mount a permanent filesystem, but as an init file, we will use a script file that will perform minimal initialization and start the sh shell.

    OS bootloading

    Over the years of its existence, Linux has been ported to many hardware platforms. The Linux boot for each platform is different. For x86, the download may differ as follows: 

    1. Will it be used to boot BIOS or UEFI? 
    2. On which storage device the BIOS or UEFI will look for the bootloader (hard drive, flash drive, optical drive, computer network)?
    3.  How the hard disk or flash drive is marked (MBR or GPT)? 
    4. On what media and in what file system (FAT, NTFS, EXT, CDFS, etc.) will the kernel file and the file with the image of the initial root filesystem, called initrd, be located?

    The structure of the initial root filesystem

    The initial root file system contains the minimum number of files and directories necessary for further Linux operation. In our case, these are the bin, dev, proc, and sys directories. The bin directory contains utilities for working with the Linux kernel.

    Utility kits

    Minimal Linux is a kernel and a set of command-line utilities. The kernel and command-line utilities are developed by different teams of programmers.

    The most common sets are:

    Due to its simplicity and low disk space, BusyBox is often used on embedded devices. I will use it for simplicity.

    Creating a Linux Build Environment

    Paradoxical as it may sound, Linux is usually compiled into Linux. To do this, you need a Linux OS, which contains programs that allow you to build the Linux kernel and a set of utilities for building.

    For example, on Ubuntu 22.10, we need to install the following packages: make, build-essential, bc, bison, flex, libssl-dev, libelf-dev, wget, cpio, fdisk, extlinux, dosfstools, and qemu-system-x86.

    Note that for other systems, the set of packages may differ.

    Building Minimal Linux on Ubuntu 22.10

    • Install the assembly-required packages. 
    $ cd ~
$ mkdir -p simple-linux/build/sources
$ mkdir -p simple-linux/build/downloads
$ mkdir -p simple-linux/build/out
$ mkdir -p simple-linux/linux
$ sudo apt update
$ sudo apt install --yes make build-essential bc bison flex libssl-dev libelf-dev wget cpio fdisk extlinux dosfstools qemu-system-x86
    • Download the source code for the Linux kernel and BusyBox from the Internet. 
    $ cd simple-linux/build
$ wget -P downloads  https://cdn.kernel.org/pub/linux/kernel/v5.x/linux-5.15.79.tar.xz
$ wget -P downloads https://busybox.net/downloads/busybox-1.35.0.tar.bz2
    • Unpack archives with source code. 
    $ tar -xvf downloads/linux-5.15.79.tar.xz -C sources
$ tar -xjvf downloads/busybox-1.35.0.tar.bz2 -C sources
    • Build BusyBox binaries for the Linux kernel as well. This process will take some time, about 10 minutes or even more, so do not be alarmed. 
    $ cd sources/busybox-1.35.0
$ make defconfig
$ make LDFLAGS=-static
$ cp busybox ../../out/
$ cd ../linux-5.15.79
$ make defconfig
$ make -j8 || exit
$ cp arch/x86_64/boot/bzImage ~/simple-linux/linux/vmlinuz-5.15.79

    $ mkdir -p ~/simple-linux/build/initrd
$ cd ~/simple-linux/build/initrd
$ vi init

    Instead of the vim editor (vi command), you can use another text editor such as gedit.

    init file:

    #! /bin/sh
mount -t sysfs sysfs /sys
mount -t proc proc /proc
mount -t devtmpfs udev /dev
sysctl -w kernel.printk="2 4 1 7"
/bin/sh
poweroff -f
    • Create the structure of directories and files. 
    $ chmod 777 init
$ mkdir -p bin dev proc sys
$ cd bin
$ cp ~/simple-linux/build/out/busybox ./
$ for prog in $(./busybox --list); do ln -s /bin/busybox $prog; done
    • Place the structure in the initrd file, which is our cpio archive. 
    $ cd ..
$ find . | cpio -o -H newc > ~/simple-linux/linux/initrd-busybox-1.35.0.img
    • Launch the built image in the QEMU emulator. 
    $ cd ~/simple-linux/linux
$ qemu-system-x86_64 -kernel vmlinuz-5.15.79 -initrd initrd-busybox-1.35.0.img -nographic -append 'console=ttyS0'
    • Let's try to enter the Linux commands you know. Exit the emulated Linux by typing the exit command.

    Create a boot image for a flash drive

    If we want to run our Linux on real hardware, then probably the easiest way is to create an image to put on a boot drive and burn it. Creating such an image and booting from the drive is, in my opinion, the most difficult process and requires more advanced knowledge from you.

    When creating an image, there are several decisions to be made:

    • What will initiate the boot (BIOS or UEFI)?
    • Which drive will you be using (CDROM, flash drive, or hard drive)?
    • How will you partition the drive (MBR, GPT), and will you partition it at all? 
    • Which bootloader will you use?
    • What file system will be used and where the Linux and bootloader files will be located?

    I used a flash drive with the MBR and EXTLINUX bootloader installed and one FAT32 partition where the files reside. The BIOS started the boot process for me (the Legacy boot option if your computer has a UEFI BIOS).

    The algorithm for creating a bootable flash drive image is as follows:

    $ dd if=/dev/zero of=boot-disk.img bs=1024K count=50
    • Create a boot partition inside the image file. 
    $ echo "type=83,bootable" | sfdisk boot-disk.img
    • Set up a loopback device on a boot partition inside the boot-disk.img file. 
    $ losetup -D
$ LOOP_DEVICE=$(losetup -f)
$ losetup -o $(expr 512 \* 2048) ${LOOP_DEVICE} boot-disk.img
    • Create a filesystem on the loopback device. 
    $ mkfs.vfat ${LOOP_DEVICE}
    • Mount the loopback device. 
    $ mkdir -p /mnt/os
$ mount -t auto ${LOOP_DEVICE} /mnt/os
    • Copy the Linux kernel file and the initrd file to the first partition inside the boot-disk.img file. 
    $ cp vmlinuz-5.15.79 initrd-busybox-1.35.0.img /mnt/os
    • In the boot-disk.img file, place the EXTLINUX bootloader.  
    $ mkdir -p /mnt/os/boot
$ extlinux --install /mnt/os/boot
    • Create a configuration file for the bootloader, in which we indicate what exactly to load. 
    $ echo "DEFAULT linux" >> /mnt/os/boot/syslinux.cfg
$ echo "  SAY Booting Simple Linux via SYSLINUX" >> /mnt/os/boot/syslinux.cfg
$ echo "  LABEL linux"  >> /mnt/os/boot/syslinux.cfg
$ echo "  KERNEL /vmlinuz-5.15.79" >> /mnt/os/boot/syslinux.cfg
$ echo "  APPEND initrd=/initrd-busybox-1.35.0.img nomodeset" >> /mnt/os/boot/syslinux.cfg
    • Unmount the loopback device. 
    $ umount /mnt/os
$ losetup -D
    • Install the MBR bootloader at the beginning of the disk inside the boot-disk.img file. 
    $ dd if=/usr/lib/syslinux/mbr/mbr.bin of=boot-disk.img bs=440 count=1 conv=notrunc

    The boot-disk.img file will contain the boot image of the flash drive.

    Using Docker to build Linux

    The algorithms described above contain many commands and parameters; it is easy to make a mistake when typing. Commands can be combined into bash scripts, and in order to be able to build Linux on the Windows 10 or 11 operating system, it is rational to use Docker Desktop.

    The essence of Docker is as follows:

    1. In a Dockerfile, you describe the environment structure for your program or script.
    2. Using the Docker utility, based on the Dockerfile, you create an image of this environment in a specific format.
    3. Using the same utility, you can launch an image-based instance of your program or script that runs in an isolated environment and is called a Docker container in Docker terminology.
    4. The images you create can be stored in a repository and reused. Docker containers created from the same image will run identically on all computers that can run them.
    5. A Dockerfile is easy to read and learn and easy to distribute.

    Below are the contents of the Dockerfile:

    FROM ubuntu:22.10
RUN apt update && apt install --yes make build-essential bc bison flex libssl-dev libelf-dev wget
RUN apt install --yes cpio fdisk extlinux dosfstools qemu-system-x86
RUN apt install --yes vim
ARG APP=/app
ARG LINUX_DIR=$APP/linux
ARG FILES_DIR=$APP/files
ARG SCRIPTS_DIR=$APP/scripts
ENV BUILD_DIR=$APP/build
ENV LINUX_DIR=$LINUX_DIR
ENV FILES_DIR=$FILES_DIR
ENV LINUX_VER=5.15.79
ENV BUSYBOX_VER=1.35.0
ENV BASH_ENV="$SCRIPTS_DIR/bash-env/env" 
COPY ./scripts $APP/scripts
COPY ./files $APP/files
RUN mkdir -p $LINUX_DIR
RUN  ln -s $APP/scripts/start-linux.sh /usr/bin/start &&\
     ln -s $APP/scripts/build-linux.sh /usr/bin/build &&\
     ln -s $APP/scripts/build-image.sh /usr/bin/image
WORKDIR $APP/scripts
CMD build

    Let's take a quick look at the main commands our Dockerfile:

    The FROM command is the most important one; it specifies which filesystem image our Linux build image will be based on. In this case, it is Ubuntu 22.10.

    The RUN command runs commands inside the image we are creating. That is, the commands that follow RUN will be executed as they would be if you were running Ubuntu 22.10 and executed them on the command line. As a result of the command, your file system image will change since these commands change the file system inside it.

    The COPY command copies files from the file system of our OS into the created image. Like RUN, it modifies the file system within the image.

    The ARG and ENV commands are confusing. I don’t know if I’ll make it clear or not, but ARG is the creation of variables that are used when creating an image, and ENV is the creation of variables that are used when a container has already been created based on this image, and these variables will be visible inside it.

    The WORKDIR command specifies which directory will be working when starting a container created based on our image.

    The CMD command specifies which command will be executed by default inside the container when it is started.

    Running and building minimal Linux with Docker

    You can experiment with my project. On Windows, it's best to run Docker in PowerShell.

    $ git clone https://github.com/artyomsoft/simple-linux.git
$ cd simple-linux
$ docker build --build-arg APP -t simple-linux .

    $ mkdir linux
$ cd linux
$ docker run -v ${pwd}:/app/linux --rm -it simple-linux build

    The linux directory you created will contain the built Linux kernel file and the initial rootsystem image file.

    • Create a boot image for the flash drive.

    Note that you need to use the --privileged option in Docker as the image script uses a loopback device.

    $ docker run -v ${pwd}:/app/linux –-privileged --rm -it simple-linux image

    If you use Docker Desktop for Linux, Docker will need to be run using sudo, and ${pwd} will need to be used instead of $(pwd).

    Burning a boot image for a flash drive to media

    The created image file for the flash drive (linux-5.15.79-busybox-1.35.0-disk.img) can be written to the flash drive using the Win32DiskImager utility.

    It should be noted that when writing, you will lose all the data stored on the flash drive, so it is better to use a drive that does not contain any files!

    After writing the image to the flash drive, restart your computer and choose to boot from USB-HDD, i.e., from the flash drive you created. Most likely, before that, you will need to select "Legacy Boot" and disable "Secure Boot" in the BIOS.

    Conclusion

    In this post, I have provided detailed instructions on how you can get a working Linux system from source code.

    To some, this post may seem very simple and not worthy of attention. But, in order not to scare you away with details, I did not delve into topics such as BIOS, UEFI, file systems, bootloaders, the glibc library, the detailed operating system boot process, various specifications, dynamic and static linking, Linux kernel modules,... I just gave the minimum amount of theory that will allow you to understand what, in fact, you did and understand the topic much faster than me, without collecting all the information bit by bit.

    You will hardly use the resulting operating system in the future, but I hope that your abstract knowledge of Linux will turn into understanding and skill for you.

    Was this post helpful? Have you ever tried to build a minimal Linux OS from the source code? Share your experience in the comments!

    Btw, you can support my work by buying me a coffee! I'll leave here a few links for you:)

    You can also support me on Coinbase
item.author: mariamarsh

item.title: 询问 AI 它认为编程语言是什么样的！ | Asking AI what it thinks programming languages look like!
item.description:
大家好，好久不见！

今天只是一个随机的有趣帖子，我回来了，不是吗？ 谁知道，但我只是想分享这个！

哦，如果你想知道我到底是谁，为什么你会收到我的东西的通知……我被称为 InHuOfficial……希望对您有所帮助！ 人工智能和编程语言

所以我最近一直在玩 AI 艺术生成，主要是通过 MidJourney AI。

一件总是很有趣的事情是，当你尝试在特定事物上生成图像时，很明显人工智能不知道那是什么。

所以我就想，AI有编程语言意识吗？ 更有趣的是，当它对某事一无所知时，它会生成什么？

这就是我的想法，我为 17 种不同的编程相关语言生成了图像，现在由您来尝试猜测哪个图像是什么语言！

玩得开心！

语言

我为 17 种与编程相关的不同语言生成了图像（请注意我是如何很好地避开调用 HTML 和 CSS 编程语言来避免争论的）。

这些都是：

  • 打字稿
  • 锈
  • 镖
  • 去
  • 迅速
  • 科特林
  • 红宝石
  • 爪哇

既然你有了这份清单，看看你能得到多少以下的东西，我已经给每一个都打了一个等级，根据我认为获得它们的难度。

图像

让我们从一个简单的开始！

难度：简单

难度：简单

难度：简单

难度：简单 - 中等

难度：中等

难度：难

难度：很容易

难度：简单

难度：简单 - 中等

难度：难

难度：中-难

难度：中-难

难度：简单

难度：中等

难度：简单

难度：简单 - 中等

难度：难

答案（剧透……呃！）

看看你答对了多少！
  1. 爪哇
  2. 镖
  3. 锈
  4. 打字稿
  5. 迅速
  6. 科特林
  7. 去
  8. 红宝石
  9. 你怎么做的？

    我希望你喜欢这篇愚蠢的帖子，让我知道你在下面的评论中猜对了多少！

    祝大家度过愉快的一周，很快再见（也许？🤫）💕



原文:

Hey all, long time no see!

Just a random fun post for you today, am I back, am I not? Who knows, but I just wanted to share this!

Oh and if you are wondering who the hell I am and why you are getting notifications for my stuff...I was known as InHuOfficial...hope that helps! 🤣💗

AI and programming languages

So I have been playing with AI art generation lately, mainly through MidJourney AI.

One thing that is always interesting is when you try and generate an image on a specific thing and it is very evident the AI has no idea what that thing is.

So I wondered, does AI have any awareness of programming languages? Even more interestingly, when it doesn't have any knowledge of something, what does it generate instead?

So that is the idea, I generated images for 17 different programming related languages and now it is up to you to try and guess which image is what language!

Have fun!

The languages

I generated images for 17 different languages related to programming (notice how I nicely side-stepped calling HTML and CSS programming languages to avoid that debate 🤣).

These are:

  • HTML
  • CSS
  • JavaScript
  • TypeScript
  • Python
  • Rust
  • Dart
  • Go
  • Swift
  • C#
  • C++
  • Kotlin
  • Ruby
  • Ruby on Rails
  • PHP
  • COBOL (haha)
  • Java

Now that you have that list, see how many of the following you can get, I have given each one a rating on MY perceived difficulty to get them.

The images

Let's start you off with an easy one!

1.

Difficulty: Easy

2.

Difficulty: Easy

3.

Difficulty: Easy

4.

Difficulty: Easy - Medium

5.

Difficulty: Medium

6.

Difficulty: Hard

7.

Difficulty: Very Easy

8.

Difficulty: Easy

9.

Difficulty: Easy - Medium

10.

Difficulty: Hard

11.

Difficulty: Medium - hard

12.

Difficulty: Medium - hard

13.

Difficulty: Easy

14.

Difficulty: Medium

15.

Difficulty: Easy

16.

Difficulty: Easy - Medium

17.

Difficulty: Hard

The answers (spoilers...duh!)

Check how many you got right!
  1. Python
  2. Java
  3. Ruby on Rails
  4. PHP
  5. C#
  6. HTML
  7. Dart
  8. Rust
  9. TypeScript
  10. CSS
  11. COBOL
  12. Swift
  13. Kotlin
  14. C++
  15. Go
  16. Ruby
  17. JavaScript

How did you do?

I hope you enjoyed this silly post, let me know how many you got right in the comments below!

Have a great week everyone, see you soon (maybe? 🤫) 💗
item.author: grahamthedev

item.title: 创建 Web 可访问性：元标记 | Creating Web Accessibility: Meta Tags
item.description:
发表于 2 月 16 日

仅仅拥有一个视觉上吸引人或美观的网站是不够的。 如果用户发现很难浏览您的页面，那就是失败。

在设计或构建网站时，必须考虑所有用户的可访问性，包括残障人士，尤其是残障人士，因为网络适合所有人。 提高网页可访问性的一种方法是在 HTML 头部部分包含元标记。 在这篇文章中，我们将讨论一些重要的可访问性元标记，您可以使用它们来改善网站的用户体验。

其中一些标签预先写在 html 模板中，但了解它们的作用和工作方式很重要。

  1. 郎
    这对于屏幕阅读器来说很重要，它可以使用语言信息为内容选择合适的语音和发音。 确保包含“lang”属性并将值设置为适当的语言代码，例如英语的“en”或西班牙语的“es”。 这是支持的语言代码列表
    示例： <html lang="en">

  2. 屏幕阅读器和其他辅助技术可以使用标题来帮助用户导航和理解页面内容。 确保包含准确代表页面内容的描述性和简洁的标题。

    示例： <title>Important Accessibility Meta Tags in HTML</title>

此标签对于可能使用辅助技术浏览搜索引擎结果的用户也很有用。 确保包含页面内容的描述性和简明摘要。

示例： <meta name="description" content="Learn about important accessibility meta tags in HTML for improving the user experience of your website.">

这对于可能使用不同设备或屏幕放大工具来查看内容的残障用户来说非常重要。 确保包含一个“视口”元标记，将页面宽度设置为设备宽度并禁用缩放。

除了可访问性之外，这些元标记还可以帮助搜索引擎和其他工具更好地理解和显示您的内容。 请记住始终遵循网络可访问性的最佳做法，以确保所有用户都可以访问您的网站。

一旦暂停，frontend_jedi 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，frontend_jedi 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，frontend_jedi 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 frontend_jedi 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 frontend_jedi 的方法：

降低 frontend_jedi 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 frontend_jedi 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Excel Bill

Posted on Feb 16

It is not enough to just have a visually appealing or aesthetically pleasing website. If users find it difficult to navigate your page, It is a failure.

when designing or building websites, it's essential to consider accessibility for all users, including those with disabilities, especially those with disabilities because the web is for everyone. One way to improve the accessibility of your web pages is to include meta tags in the HTML head section. In this post, we'll discuss a few important accessibility meta tags that you can use to improve the user experience of your website.

some of these tags come pre-written in the html template, but it's important to konw what they do and how they work.

  1. Lang
    The "lang" attribute in the HTML tag specifies the language of the web page. This is important for screen readers, which can use the language information to select the appropriate voice and pronunciation for the content. Make sure to include the "lang" attribute and set the value to the appropriate language code, such as "en" for English or "es" for Spanish. Here is a list of supported language codes
    Example: <html lang="en">

  2. title The "title" element in the head section of the HTML page provides a short, descriptive title for the page. Screen readers and other assistive technologies can use the title to help users navigate and understand the content of the page. Make sure to include a descriptive and concise title that accurately represents the content of the page.

    Example: <title>Important Accessibility Meta Tags in HTML</title>

description The "description" meta tag provides a brief summary of the content of the page, which can be used by search engines and other tools to display a preview or snippet of the page. This tag can also be useful for users who may use assistive technology to browse search engine results. Make sure to include a descriptive and concise summary of the page's content.

Example: <meta name="description" content="Learn about important accessibility meta tags in HTML for improving the user experience of your website.">

viewport The "viewport" meta tag helps to ensure that the web page is properly displayed on different devices and screen sizes. This is important for users with disabilities who may use different devices or screen magnification tools to view the content. Make sure to include a "viewport" meta tag that sets the width of the page to the device width and disables zooming.

Example: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no">

Aside from accessibility, these meta tags can also help search engines and other tools better understand and display your content. Remember to always follow best practices for web accessibility to ensure that your website is accessible to all users.

☝️ Check out this all-time classic DEV post

Once suspended, frontend_jedi will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, frontend_jedi will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by frontend_jedi will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If frontend_jedi is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag frontend_jedi:

Make all posts by frontend_jedi less visible

frontend_jedi consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging frontend_jedi will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 机器学习中的过拟合和欠拟合 | Overfitting and Underfitting in Machine learning
item.description:
机器学习算法用于根据过去的示例进行预测、识别模式和趋势以及对数据进行分类。 但是，为了创建能够准确预测未来数据的模型，必须平衡模型可用于学习的信息量与它可概括的信息量。 过度拟合和欠拟合是训练机器学习模型时常见的两个问题。

过拟合

过度拟合发生在模型过于复杂时，它对训练数据的学习太好，以至于它变得过于特定于训练数据并且无法泛化到新数据。 这导致模型在训练数据上非常准确，但在新的、未见过的数据上表现不佳。

过度拟合的最常见原因之一是模型相对于训练数据集的大小具有太多特征或参数。 发生这种情况时，模型可能过于贴近训练数据而无法推广到新数据。 过度拟合的另一个原因是当模型训练了太多次迭代时，导致它学习训练数据中的噪声而不是底层模式。

例如，让我们考虑一个模型，该模型经过训练可以根据患者的年龄、BMI、血压和血糖水平等多种特征来预测患者是否患有糖尿病。 如果模型太复杂，它可能会学习特定于训练数据的模式，例如具有特定年龄范围和血压的患者更可能患有糖尿病。 但是，这可能无法推广到新数据，其中的模式可能不同。

欠拟合

当模型过于简单并且无法捕获训练数据中的潜在模式时，就会发生欠拟合。 这会导致模型在训练数据或新数据上都不准确。

欠拟合的最常见原因之一是模型相对于问题的复杂性而言具有太少的特征或参数。 发生这种情况时，模型可能无法捕获数据中的基本模式。 欠拟合的另一个原因是模型训练时间不够长或没有足够的数据，导致它错过重要的模式。

例如，让我们考虑一个模型，该模型经过训练可以根据房屋大小、卧室数量和房屋使用年限来预测房价。 如果模型过于简单，可能无法捕捉到这些特征与房价之间的复杂关系，导致模型不准确。

过拟合和欠拟合的解决方案

有几种技术可用于解决机器学习中的过度拟合和欠拟合问题：

  1. 正则化：正则化是一种通过向模型的损失函数添加惩罚项来防止过度拟合的技术。 这个惩罚项鼓励模型使用更简单的权重和偏差，这反过来又有助于它更好地泛化到新数据。

  2. 交叉验证：交叉验证是一种用于评估模型在多个数据子集上的性能的技术。 通过在不同的数据子集上测试模型，可以确定模型是过度拟合还是欠拟合。

  3. 这有助于防止模型学习训练数据中的噪声。

  4. 数据扩充：数据扩充是一种用于通过转换现有数据创建新示例来增加训练数据量的技术。 这可以通过为模型提供更多可供学习的示例来帮助防止过度拟合。

  5. 特征选择：特征选择是一种用于从数据集中选择最重要的特征并删除任何不相关特征的技术。 这有助于降低模型的复杂性并防止过度拟合。

  6. 集成方法：集成方法涉及组合多个模型以提高其性能。 通过使用具有不同优势和劣势的多个模型，可以创建一个更强大的模型来处理过拟​​合和欠拟合。

结论

过拟合和欠拟合是机器学习中的常见问题，但有几种技术可以用来解决这些问题。 通过使用正则化、交叉验证、提前停止、数据增强、特征选择和集成方法，可以创建更准确且能够更好地泛化到新数据的模型。 了解这些概念和技术对于任何从事机器学习领域工作的人来说都是必不可少的，并且可以帮助确保他们的模型尽可能准确和有效。



原文:

Machine learning algorithms are used to make predictions, identify patterns and trends, and classify data based on past examples. However, in order to create models that accurately predict future data, it's essential to balance the amount of information that a model can use to learn with the amount of information that it can generalize. Overfitting and underfitting are two common problems that occur when training machine learning models.

Overfitting

Overfitting occurs when a model is too complex, and it has learned the training data too well, to the point that it becomes overly specific to the training data and is unable to generalize to new data. This results in a model that is very accurate on the training data, but performs poorly on new, unseen data.

One of the most common causes of overfitting is when the model has too many features or parameters relative to the size of the training dataset. When this occurs, the model may fit the training data too closely and fail to generalize to new data. Another cause of overfitting is when the model is trained for too many iterations, causing it to learn the noise in the training data rather than the underlying patterns.

For example, let's consider a model that is trained to predict whether a patient has diabetes based on several features, such as their age, BMI, blood pressure, and glucose levels. If the model is too complex, it may learn patterns that are specific to the training data, such as the fact that patients with a particular age range and blood pressure are more likely to have diabetes. However, this may not generalize to new data, where the patterns may be different.

Underfitting

Underfitting occurs when a model is too simple and is unable to capture the underlying patterns in the training data. This results in a model that is not accurate on either the training data or new data.

One of the most common causes of underfitting is when the model has too few features or parameters relative to the complexity of the problem. When this occurs, the model may not be able to capture the underlying patterns in the data. Another cause of underfitting is when the model is not trained for long enough or with enough data, causing it to miss important patterns.

For example, let's consider a model that is trained to predict housing prices based on the size of the house, the number of bedrooms, and the age of the house. If the model is too simple, it may not capture the complex relationship between these features and the price of the house, resulting in a model that is inaccurate.

Solutions to Overfitting and Underfitting

There are several techniques that can be used to address overfitting and underfitting in machine learning:

  1. Regularization: Regularization is a technique used to prevent overfitting by adding a penalty term to the loss function of the model. This penalty term encourages the model to use simpler weights and biases, which in turn helps it to generalize better to new data.

  2. Cross-validation: Cross-validation is a technique used to evaluate a model's performance on multiple subsets of the data. By testing the model on different subsets of the data, it is possible to identify whether the model is overfitting or underfitting.

  3. Early stopping: Early stopping is a technique used to prevent overfitting by stopping the training process when the model's performance on a validation set stops improving. This helps to prevent the model from learning the noise in the training data.

  4. Data augmentation: Data augmentation is a technique used to increase the amount of training data by creating new examples through transformations of the existing data. This can help to prevent overfitting by giving the model more examples to learn from.

  5. Feature selection: Feature selection is a technique used to select the most important features from a dataset and remove any irrelevant features. This helps to reduce the complexity of the model and prevent overfitting.

  6. Ensemble methods: Ensemble methods involve combining multiple models to improve their performance. By using multiple models with different strengths and weaknesses, it is possible to create a more robust model that can handle both overfitting and underfitting.

Conclusion

Overfitting and underfitting are common problems in machine learning, but there are several techniques that can be used to address them. By using regularization, cross-validation, early stopping, data augmentation, feature selection, and ensemble methods, it is possible to create models that are more accurate and better able to generalize to new data. Understanding these concepts and techniques is essential for anyone working in the field of machine learning, and can help to ensure that their models are as accurate and effective as possible.

item.title: 如何选择网站开发公司？ | How to Choose a Web Development Company?
item.description:
维罗妮卡·罗斯

发表于 2 月 16 日


在构建网站时，选择合适的网络开发公司对于项目的成功至关重要。 合适的网络开发公司可以帮助您创建满足您的需求和受众需求的专业、高质量的网站。 然而，有这么多可供选择的选项，为您的项目找到合适的网络开发公司可能具有挑战性。 在本文中，我们将为您提供如何选择网络开发公司的正确定义，并提供一些提示来帮助您做出正确的选择。

首先，了解什么是 Web 开发很重要。 它涉及各种任务，包括前端开发、后端开发（处理服务器端编程和数据库管理）和全栈开发。

在选择网络开发公司时，需要考虑几个因素。 这里有一些提示可以帮助您做出正确的选择：

  1. 定义您的项目要求：在您开始寻找网络开发公司之前，定义您的项目要求很重要。 您希望您的网站做什么？ 谁是您的目标受众？ 你的预算是多少？ 清楚地了解您的项目要求将帮助您缩小选择范围并找到最适合满足您需求的网络开发公司。

  2. 寻找经验和专业知识：在选择网络开发公司时，重要的是要寻找在您的行业中具有经验和专业知识的公司。 查看公司的投资组合，看看他们是否从事过与您类似的项目。 询问参考资料并查看在线评论，看看其他客户对他们在公司工作的经历有何评价。

  3. 考虑沟通和项目管理：沟通和项目管理是任何 Web 开发项目成功的关键因素。 确保您选择的网络开发公司制定了清晰有效的沟通策略，并且他们会在整个开发过程中随时通知您。 此外，询问他们的项目管理流程以及他们将如何确保您的项目在预算内按时交付。

  4. 寻找提供持续支持和维护的公司：一旦您的网站上线，您将需要持续的支持和维护以确保它保持最新和安全。 寻找提供持续支持和维护服务的网络开发公司，并确保这些服务包含在您的合同中。

总之，

选择合适的 Web 开发公司对于项目的成功至关重要。 确保定义项目要求，寻找经验和专业知识，考虑沟通和项目管理，并寻找提供持续支持和维护的公司。 牢记这些提示，您将能够找到最适合满足您的需求并帮助您实现目标的网络开发公司。
欲了解更多信息，请联系我们🚀

原文:

Veronica Ross

Posted on Feb 16


When it comes to building a website, choosing the right web development company is crucial to the success of your project. The right web development company can help you create a professional, high-quality website that meets your needs and the needs of your audience. However, with so many options to choose from, it can be challenging to find the right web development company for your project. In this article, we will provide you with a proper definition of how to choose a web development company and offer some tips to help you make the right choice.

First and foremost, it's important to understand what web development is. Web development is the process of designing, building, and maintaining websites. It involves a variety of tasks, including front-end development, back-end development (which deals with the server-side programming and database management), and full-stack development.

When it comes to choosing a web development company, there are several factors to consider. Here are some tips to help you make the right choice:

  1. Define your project requirements: Before you start searching for a web development company, it's important to define your project requirements. What do you want your website to do? Who is your target audience? What is your budget? Having a clear understanding of your project requirements will help you narrow down your options and find a web development company that is best suited to meet your needs.

  2. Look for experience and expertise: When choosing a web development company, it's important to look for a company with experience and expertise in your industry. Look at the company's portfolio and see if they have worked on projects similar to yours. Ask for references and check online reviews to see what other clients are saying about their experience working with the company.

  3. Consider communication and project management: Communication and project management are key factors in the success of any web development project. Make sure the web development company you choose has a clear and effective communication strategy in place, and that they will keep you informed throughout the development process. Also, ask about their project management process and how they will ensure that your project is delivered on time and within budget.

  4. Look for a company that offers ongoing support and maintenance: Once your website is live, you will need ongoing support and maintenance to ensure that it remains up-to-date and secure. Look for a web development company that offers ongoing support and maintenance services, and make sure that these services are included in your contract.

In Summary,

Choosing the right web development company is crucial to the success of your project. Make sure you define your project requirements, look for experience and expertise, consider communication and project management, and look for a company that offers ongoing support and maintenance. With these tips in mind, you'll be able to find a web development company that is best suited to meet your needs and help you achieve your goals.
For more information, Please Contact us 🚀

Git Concepts I Wish I Knew Years Ago

The most used technology by developers is not Javascript.

It's not Python or HTML.

It hardly even gets mentioned in interviews or listed as a pre-requisite for jobs.

I'm talking about Git and version control of course.

item.title: Solana 如何忽略安全最佳实践 | How Solana Ignores Security Best Practices
item.description:
我真的认为在加密和开源中“我看到了一切”。

但是，今天，索拉纳让我大吃一惊。

我访问了 github ( github.com/solana-labs/solana ) 上的项目，并试图大致了解约 800 个开放问题和约 100 个开放 PR。

太多了，所以我专注于较旧的问题，将其进一步缩小到security问题。

令我惊讶的是，我被...封锁了：

甚至像这样的问题

保持打开状态。 团队可能知道这些问题并不重要。

但是访客不能确定。

我想这就是团队在财务上取得成功后会发生的事情：他们只是随心所欲，在（他们的开源代码库的）访问者有顾虑时开玩笑。

这一切太令人失望了。

仍然需要找到一个智能合约平台，核心开发人员尽管在财务上取得了成功，但仍能保持一定的（技术、程序）理智。

暂停后，abebeos 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，abebeos 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，abebeos 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 abebeos 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

原文:

I really thought "I saw it all" within crypto and open-source.

But then, today, Solana managed to surprise me.

I visited the project on github (github.com/solana-labs/solana), and tried to get an overview of the ~800 open issues and ~100 open PRs.

Far too much, so I focused on the older issues, narrowed it further down to security issues.

To my surprise, I was... blocked:

github.com/solana-labs/solana/issues/30…

Even issues like

Potential privilege escalation in sys-tuner
github.com/solana-labs/solana/issues/91…

are left open. The team maybe knows that the issues are non-critical.

But a visitor cannot be sure.

I guess that this is what happens after a team is successful financially: they simply do as it pleases them, joking around when visitors (of their open-source code-base) have concerns.

So disappointing all this.

Still need to find a smart-contract platform where the core-devs have kept some (technological, procedural) sanity, despite their financial success.

.

Pachi 🪐 (she/her/ela) - Jan 17

item.title: 面向对象编程 | Object-Oriented Programming
item.description:
拉维香卡

发表于 2 月 16 日

类是创建对象的蓝图或原型。它包含一些属性和方法。

 class Person: def __init__(self, name): self.name = name def talk(self): print(f"Hey, I am {self.name}")

目的

对象是具有与其关联的状态和行为的实体。 它可以是任何真实世界的对象，如桌子、狗、汽车等。 对象中有属性和方法。

 class Person: def __init__(self, name): self.name = name def talk(self): print(f"Hey, I am {self.name}") p_1 = Person('raina') print(p_1.name) p_1.talk()

它在创建类的对象时执行。 该方法可用于对对象进行任何初始化。

自己

遗产

继承是一个类从另一个类派生或继承属性的能力。 派生属性的类称为派生类或子类，从中派生属性的类称为基类或父类。
继承类型：
单继承单级继承使派生类能够继承单父类的特性。

 # parent class class Person: def __init__(self, name, age): self.name = name self.age = age def details(self): print(f"I am {self.name}") print(f"My id is {self.age}") # child class class Employee(Person): def __init__(self, name, age, pay, job): self.pay = pay self.job = job Person.__init__(self,name, age) def details(self): super().details() print(f"My job is {self.job}") print(f"My salary is {self.pay}") Emp_1 = Employee('raina',24,10000,"Software") Emp_1.details()

多级继承多级继承使派生类能够从直接父类继承属性，而直接父类又从其父类继承属性。

 class Person: def __init__(self, name, age): self.name = name self.age = age def details(self): print(f"I am {self.name}") print(f"My id is {self.age}") def fun(self): print("Inside Person class") class Employee(Person): def __init__(self,name,age,pay, job): self.pay = pay self.job = job Person.__init__(self,name,age) def details(self): super().details() print(f"My job is {self.job}") print(f"My salary is {self.pay}") def run(self): print("Inside Employee class") class Manager(Employee): def __init__(self,name,age,pay,job,exp): self.exp = exp Employee.__init__(self,name,age,pay,job) def details(self): super().details() print(f"Experience : {self.exp}") Man_1 = Manager('raina',24,1000,"Software",5) Man_1.details() Man_1.fun() Man_1.run()

层次继承层次继承使多个派生类能够从父类继承属性。

 class Vehical: def __init__(self,wheels,seats,doors): self.wheels = wheels self.seats = seats self.doors = doors def horn(self): print("Honk Honk") def start(self): print("Started") def stop(self): print("Stoped") class Car(Vehical): def __init__(self,wheels,seats,doors): Vehical.__init__(self,wheels,seats,doors) def display(self): print("It is a car") class Bus(Vehical): def __init__(self,wheels,seats,doors): Vehical.__init__(self,wheels,seats,doors) def display(self): print("It is a Bus") omini = Car(4,6,4) bus1 = Bus(6,20,2) omini.start() bus1.start()

多态性

多态性定义了采用不同形式的能力。 方法覆盖

class A: def display(self): print("Inside A class") class B(A): def display(self): print("Inside class B") a1 = B() a1.display()

这里子类继承了它的父类方法，但它通过这样做对子类中的方法进行了更改，我们用子类显示方法覆盖了父类显示方法。

封装

将变量和方法包装到单个实体中的过程称为封装。它充当保护罩，限制直接访问变量和方法。

使用双下划线 (__variablename) 使变量私有，使用单下划线 (_variablename) 使其受保护。

可以在同一个类中访问私有变量。
可以在同一个类和子类中访问受保护的变量。

封装有一些好处，其中一些是：

  • 数据隐藏
  • 灵活性
  • 可重用性
class Tech: def __init__(self, name, course): self.name = name # private variable self.__course = course def set__course(self,c): self.__course = c def get__course(self): return self.__course t1 = Tech('Raina', 'Python') print("Name:",t1.name) # accessing private variable __course using # _Tech__course name print("Course:",t1._Tech__course) #accessing private variable using setter and getter methods t1.set__course('Java') print("Course:",t1.get__course())

抽象

抽象是指隐藏不必要的细节以专注于整个产品，而不是单独关注项目的各个部分。 它是一种表示重要功能但不包括实现细节的机制。 抽象帮助我们将程序划分为许多独立的概念，这样我们就可以在代码中隐藏不相关的信息。 在不同情况下使用抽象数据类型对象时，它提供了最大的灵活性。

 from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class Vehicle(ABC): @abstractmethod def start(self): pass class Car(Vehicle): def start(self): print("Car has started") nano = Car() nano.start()

参考：
精选的 70 个基于 Web 的实用工具的集合。
每个都将生成纯 CSS，而不需要 JS 或任何外部库。

受保护的港口 - 2 月 15 日

一旦取消发布，ravishankar 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

报告其他不当行为

原文:

Ravi Shankar

Posted on Feb 16

A class is the blueprint or the prototype from which the objects are being created.It contains some attributes and methods.

class Person: def __init__(self, name): self.name = name def talk(self): print(f"Hey, I am {self.name}")

Object

The object is an entity that has a state and behavior associated with it. It may be any real-world object like a table,dog,car,etc. An object has attributes and methods in it.

class Person: def __init__(self, name): self.name = name def talk(self): print(f"Hey, I am {self.name}") p_1 = Person('raina')
print(p_1.name)
p_1.talk()

__init__ method

The __init__ method is similar to constructor. It is executed as an object of a class is created. The method is useful to do any initialization to the object.

self

self represents the instance of a class. self allows to access variables,attributes,and methods of a defined class in python.

self must always be the first argument of any class method.

Inheritance

Inheritance is the capability of one class to derive or inherit the properties from another class. The class that derives properties is called the derived class or child class and the class from which the properties are being derived is called the base class or parent class.
Types of Inheritance:
Single Inheritance Single-level inheritance enables a derived class to inherit characteristics from a single-parent class.

# parent class
class Person: def __init__(self, name, age): self.name = name self.age = age def details(self): print(f"I am {self.name}") print(f"My id is {self.age}") # child class
class Employee(Person): def __init__(self, name, age, pay, job): self.pay = pay self.job = job Person.__init__(self,name, age) def details(self): super().details() print(f"My job is {self.job}") print(f"My salary is {self.pay}") Emp_1 = Employee('raina',24,10000,"Software")
Emp_1.details()

Multilevel Inheritance Multi-level inheritance enables a derived class to inherit properties from an immediate parent class which in turn inherits properties from his parent class.

class Person: def __init__(self, name, age): self.name = name self.age = age def details(self): print(f"I am {self.name}") print(f"My id is {self.age}") def fun(self): print("Inside Person class") class Employee(Person): def __init__(self,name,age,pay, job): self.pay = pay self.job = job Person.__init__(self,name,age) def details(self): super().details() print(f"My job is {self.job}") print(f"My salary is {self.pay}") def run(self): print("Inside Employee class") class Manager(Employee): def __init__(self,name,age,pay,job,exp): self.exp = exp Employee.__init__(self,name,age,pay,job) def details(self): super().details() print(f"Experience : {self.exp}") Man_1 = Manager('raina',24,1000,"Software",5)
Man_1.details()
Man_1.fun()
Man_1.run()

Hierarchical Inheritance Hierarchical level inheritance enables more than one derived class to inherit properties from a parent class.

class Vehical: def __init__(self,wheels,seats,doors): self.wheels = wheels self.seats = seats self.doors = doors def horn(self): print("Honk Honk") def start(self): print("Started") def stop(self): print("Stoped") class Car(Vehical): def __init__(self,wheels,seats,doors): Vehical.__init__(self,wheels,seats,doors) def display(self): print("It is a car") class Bus(Vehical): def __init__(self,wheels,seats,doors): Vehical.__init__(self,wheels,seats,doors) def display(self): print("It is a Bus") omini = Car(4,6,4)
bus1 = Bus(6,20,2)
omini.start()
bus1.start()

Multiple Inheritance Multiple level inheritance enables one derived class to inherit properties from more than one base class.

class Alpha: def SayHi(self): print("Hi inside Alpha class") class Beta: def SayBye(self): print("Bye from Beta class") class Gamma(Alpha,Beta): def display(self): print("Welcome to Gamma class") super().SayHi() super().SayBye() a1 = Gamma()
a1.display()

Polymorphism

Polymorphism defines the ability to take different forms. Polymorphism in Python allows us to define methods in the child class with the same name as defined in their parent class.

Method Overriding

class A: def display(self): print("Inside A class") class B(A): def display(self): print("Inside class B") a1 = B()
a1.display()

Here child class inherited it's parent class method but it made changes to the method in child class by doing that we are overriding the parent class display method with the child class display method.

Encapsulation

The process of wrapping up variables and methods into a single entity is known as Encapsulation.It acts as a protective shield that puts restrictions on accessing variables and methods directly.

Using double underscore (__variablename) to make a variable private and single underscore (_variablename) to make it protected.

Private variables can be accessed within the same class.
Protected variables can be accessed within the same class and sub classes.

Encapsulation has some benefits, some of which are :

  • Data Hiding
  • Flexibility
  • Reusability 
class Tech: def __init__(self, name, course): self.name = name # private variable
 self.__course = course def set__course(self,c): self.__course = c def get__course(self): return self.__course t1 = Tech('Raina', 'Python')
print("Name:",t1.name) # accessing private variable __course using # _Tech__course name
print("Course:",t1._Tech__course) #accessing private variable using setter and getter methods
t1.set__course('Java')
print("Course:",t1.get__course())

Abstraction

Abstraction refers to hiding unnecessary details to focus on the whole product instead of parts of the project separately. It is a mechanism that represents the important features without including implementation details. Abstraction helps us in partitioning the program into many independent concepts so we may hide the irrelevant information in the code. It offers the greatest flexibility when using abstract data-type objects in different situations.


from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class Vehicle(ABC): @abstractmethod def start(self): pass class Car(Vehicle): def start(self): print("Car has started") nano = Car()
nano.start()

References:
Python Documentation
OOPs in python by freecodecamp

item.title: Python 技术论文（数组和字符串） | Python Technical Paper (Arrays and Strings)
item.description:
作为程序员，我们总是知道 python 中的一切都是一个“对象”，包括但不限于函数。 这一事实成为 python 库和定义（内置和用户定义）存储方式的支柱和基础。 这个想法将在各种例子的帮助下得到证明。 另一件需要注意的事情是处理可变和不可变对象以及围绕这些对象的任何操作的概念。 列表对象被称为“可变的”，而字符串被视为“不可变的”。 这种区别对这些类型的数据存储元素的使用有很多影响。 本文的后续部分将深入探讨基本概念、设置、细微差别以及两个对象的演示。

可变/不可变：

在了解 python 可以为程序员提供的用于处理列表和字符串数据类型的工具集之前，了解引用在 python 中的工作方式非常重要。

考虑以下代码片段及其功能。

我们经常说字符串和 int 数据类型是不可变的，理解两者之间区别的一个好方法是借助“id”函数。 此函数返回存储我们的数据的内存位置。 当我们用名称“x”声明一个变量并为其分配一个整数值时，python 解释器在内存中分配空间并存储起始值 35。现在假设我们要将存储在变量 x 中的值更改为不同的值，比如 36 ， 会发生什么？ 正如您在“id”的帮助下看到的那样，我们注意到 python 在空间中分配了一个单独的内存，用于将值 36 存储在名为“x”的变量中。 我们也可以说 Python 决定创建一个新对象并存储值 36 并将名称“x”分配给该对象。

看待不变性的另一个角度是借助下一个字符串值“Test”的示例。 我们声明了 2 个名为“a”和“b”的对象，并分配了值“Test”。 检查两个对象的 id 后，我们发现它们具有相同的 id。

现在考虑这个片段并注意“id”。

这里我们声明相同的列表，就像字符串一样，并检查它们的 id 以查看是否有任何不同。 当然这是不同的。 即使将相同的列表传递给两个对象变量，“id”的这种差异也是可变性和不可变性方面所有差异的基础，并扩展到字符串和列表之间的方法执行。

列出方法

我们现在将看到程序员喜欢在一致的基础上使用的一些更常用的列表方法。 所有列表方法的通用语法框架遵循以下格式：

 list_name.method_name(arguments)

此方法用于将元素添加到列表的末尾序列。 请参阅图像以了解附加的工作原理。

  • 插入（）

    此方法的操作类似于附加，除了用户可以根据他希望在哪个索引中插入值来自由添加值。

这里我们在列表中的索引值 2 处插入元素 6。

  • 流行音乐（）

    此方法用于根据提供的索引参数从列表中返回和删除元素。 在这里强调 return 这个词，因为在某些情况下，用户可能希望保留在 pop 操作期间删除的元素。

请注意 pop 函数如何根据是否提供索引参数来改变其行为。

  • 撤销（）

    此方法用于反转列表中元素的顺序。

注意所有操作中的 id 如何保持相同，因为操作是在同一个对象上执行的。

  • 种类（）

    此方法用于根据提供的参数列表对列表元素执行就地（在对象内存位置内）排序。

参数列表允许用户在关键字“key”的帮助下对列表进行排序或设置其他排序标准。

  • 指数（）

    此方法用于检测并返回方法语法符号中第一个参数首次出现的索引，如果提供则起始搜索位置为参数 2，如果未提供则从索引 = 0 开始。

请注意，在第一种情况下，在为列表中的搜索元素 3 提供起始位置作为索引 5 时，我们会得到一个 ValueError，指出元素 3 在索引 5 之后的列表中不存在。

  • 数数（）

    此方法用于根据提供的参数查找列表中某个元素出现的总次数。

字符串方法：

我们现在将看到程序员喜欢在一致的基础上使用的一些更常用的字符串方法。

  • 以。。开始（）

    此方法用于返回字符串是否以某个特定字符串序列开头，该字符串序列作为参数提供以测试以及起始位置和结束位置（如果存在）。 起始位置的默认值为 0。

  • 以。。结束（）

    此方法用于返回字符串是否以某个特定的字符串序列结尾，该序列作为参数提供以进行测试以及起始位置和结束位置（如果存在）。 起始位置的默认值是字符串的最后位置。

  • 数数（）

    此方法用于返回字符串中字符的总数，该字符串作为参数提供给测试以及起始位置和结束位置（如果存在）。 起始位置的默认值为 0。

  • 指数（）

    此方法用于返回字符串中字符首次出现的索引，该字符作为参数提供给测试以及起始位置和结束位置（如果存在）。 起始位置的默认值为 0。

  • 此方法用于返回字符串中是否存在字母数字字符。

  • 是数字（）

    此方法用于返回完整字符串中仅存在数字字符。

  • 此方法用于返回字符串中是否存在大写字符。

  • 此方法用于返回字符串中是否存在小写字符。

  • 分裂（）

    此方法用于根据提供的分隔符参数返回可迭代数据。 默认分隔符是空格 (' ')。

结论：

我希望到最后，您不仅可以熟悉这些方法的功能，还可以理解如果您正在处理可变或不可变数据类型，那么它们的结构为何如此基于它们。



原文:

As programmers, we always here this fact that everything in python is an "object" including and not limited to functions as well. This very fact becomes the backbone and the basis for how the python libraries and definitions (in-built and user-defined) are stored. This idea is what will be demonstrated with the help of various examples. Another thing to note is the concept of dealing with mutable and immutable objects and any operations surrounding those objects. List objects are said to be "mutable" and strings are treated as "immutable". This distinction has lot of implications in the usage of these types of data storage elements. Following section of this paper will dive into the basic idea, setup, nuances along with demonstrations for both objects.

Mutable / Immutable:

Before going into the set of tools that python can provide to programmers for working with lists and string data types, it is very important to understand how referencing works in python.

Consider the following code snippet and how it functions.

We often say that strings and int data types are immutable and a nice way to understand the difference between the two is with the help of the "id" function. This function returns the memory location wherein our data is being stored. When we declare a variable by the name "x" and assign it an integer value, the python interpreter allocates space in memory and stores the starting value 35. Now suppose we were to change the value stored in the variable x to something different say 36, what will happen? Well as you can see with the help of "id", we notice that python allocated a separate memory in space for storing the value 36 in the variable named "x". We can also say that Python decided to create a new object and store the value 36 and assigned the name "x" to the object.

Another angle of looking at immutability is with the help of the next example with the string value "Test". We declare 2 objects named 'a' and 'b' and assign the value "Test". Upon checking the id for both objects, we see that both have the same id.

Consider this snippet now and notice the "id".

Here we are declaring the same list, like with strings, and checking their id to see if anything is different. And of course it is different. This difference in "id" even when passing the same list to both object variables is the basis of all the differences in terms of mutability and immutability and extends to method executions between strings and lists.

List Methods

We shall now see some of the more commonly used list methods that programmers like to use on a consistent basis. The general syntax skeleton for all list methods follows the format:

list_name.method_name(arguments)

This method is used to add elements to the end sequence of the list. Refer to the image to understand how append works.

  • insert()

    This method operates like append, except the user has the freedom to add value based on which index he wishes to insert the value in.

Here we are inserting the element 6 in our list at index value 2.

  • pop()

    This method is used to return and remove elements from a list based on what index argument is provided. Stress on the word return here, since in some cases the user might want to retain what element it was that removed during the pop operation.

Notice how pop function alters its behavior based on when an index argument is provided or not.

  • reverse()

    This method is used to reverse the order of the elements in a list.

Notice how the id through all the operations remains the same since the operations are being performed on the same object.

  • sort()

    This method is used for performing in-place(within the object memory location) sorting of elements of a list based on the argument list provided.

The argument list allows the user to either revere sort the list or set other criteria for sorting with the help of the keyword "key".

  • index()

    This method is used for detecting and returning the index of first occurrence of the first argument in the method syntax notation with the starting search position as argument 2 if provided or from index = 0 if not provided.

Notice how in the first case upon providing the start position as index 5 for search element 3 in the list, we get a ValueError stating that element 3 is not present in the list after index 5.

  • count()

    This method is used for finding the total count of an occurrence of an element in a list based on the argument provided.

String Methods:

We shall now see some of the more commonly used string methods that programmers like to use on a consistent basis.

  • startswith()

    This method is used to return if a string starts with a certain specific string sequence which is provided as argument to test along with starting position and end position if present. The default value for starting position is 0.

  • endswith()

    This method is used to return if a string ends with a certain specific string sequence which is provided as argument to test along with starting position and end position if present. The default value for starting position is last position of the string.

  • count()

    This method is used to return the total count of a character in a string which is provided as argument to test along with starting position and end position if present. The default value for starting position is 0.

  • index()

    This method is used to return the index of first occurrence of a character in a string which is provided as argument to test along with starting position and end position if present. The default value for starting position is 0.

  • isalnum()

    This method is used to return presence of alphanumeric characters in a string.

  • isdigit()

    This method is used to return presence of only numeric characters in the complete string.

  • isupper()

    This method is used to return presence of upper-case characters in a string.

  • islower()

    This method is used to return presence of lower-case characters in a string.

  • split()

    This method is used to return an iterable data based on the delimiter argument provided. The default delimiter is space (' ').

Conclusion:

I hope that by the end of this, you not only get well acquainted with how the methods function but also understand why they are structured the way they are based on if you are dealing with a mutable or immutable data type.
item.author: curioushugo

item.title: 为什么大型现场服务企业优先考虑定制化 | Why do large Field Service Enterprises Give Priority to Customization
item.description:
分散的业务环境使定制成为必需品，而不是一种选择。 坚持严格流程并提供一刀切的服务的企业已经过时了。

当今的商业生态系统发展迅速。 客户的情绪和需求变化无常且变化迅速。 企业通过创新来提供差异化​​服务并应对这些变化。 这种差异化通常需要定制堆栈。 僵化的业务流程让外勤人员陷入常规。 他们习惯了一个僵化的过程，无法改变。 大型服务公司设计灵活的商业模式，根据每个客户的需求做出改变。 彻底改革他们的流程以关注客户需求。 流程效率变得服从于客户的偏好。

以客户为中心的系统捕获特定的客户需求和偏好并生成工作流。 工单记录了客户提出的独特需求。 工作订单与库存管理套件同步，以检查零件可用性。 自动订购系统会订购任何缺少的零件，并让客户了解可用性。 一旦零件和称职的技术人员到位，系统就会派遣现场代理来完成工作。 现场代理应用程序包含所有相关详细信息，包括到达工地的最佳路线、工作单副本和服务历史记录。 现场服务代理可能会根据现场情况更改工作单。


支持人工智能的调度和调度为这些关键流程提供了活力。 人工智能驱动的算法考虑了客户的特殊需求。 它将客户需求与劳动力可用性相匹配，并有效地安排时间。 调度时，系统实时生成最佳路线。 如果客户取消或重新安排约会，系统会找到一个新时段。 前往工作途中的技术人员得到了一份替代工作。

更加注重预防性维护

维护变得越来越具有预防性。 更多的企业更喜欢年度合同，通过例行检查来预防停机和中断。 物联网的进步使传感器能够连接到设备并获得实时警报。 当燃油不足或重要运行参数偏离正常范围时，传感器会向指挥中心发出警报。 此类警报使现场服务人员能够及时进行预防性维护。 与被动修复相比，先发制人的问题成本更低，破坏性更小。 成功的预防性维护需要改变现场服务的方法。 支持业务的现场服务必须更加以​​客户为导向。 他们必须调整服务以满足不同客户的需求。 提供通用包并期望客户适应是站不住脚的。 对灵活性和弹性的需求 今天的客户需要快速的服务和透明度。 他们具有较高的认知度，并期望服务代表能够与他们的认知度相匹配。 他们还希望按照自己的条件、在自己选择的时间并通过自己选择的渠道参与。 如果遇到延迟或服务质量差，他们会去别处 现场服务业务将用户细分以实现定制。 创建用户配置文件可以轻松地为每个客户定制体验。 例如，为优质客户付费以获得卓越体验的个人资料可以轻松提供调度优先权。 启用这种基于配置文件的定制需要改变方法。 现场服务必须将定制集成为正常工作流程的一部分。 将定制视为附加服务或对客户的帮助是行不通的。

今天的客户也经常改变主意。 有时，由于上游或下游工作流程的变化，或者由于不断变化的业务条件，他们需要进行更改。 大型企业的调度员和调度员管理着大量的现场工作人员。 这些驻扎在不同地方的外勤特工具有不同的技能。 依靠人为判断和人工干预来根据客户要求调整工作分配会导致混淆。 这种混淆会降低生产力并危及现场人员的安全。 它还会降低客户体验。 通过现场服务软件处理此类请求可实现无缝定制。

取悦内部客户

许多企业在定义客户时犯了严重的错误。 外部客户带来收入，应该始终是首要关注点。 但现场服务还有另一组内部客户——现场代理或技术人员。 现场代理是最终客户与企业之间直接且通常是唯一的接口。 满足内部客户可以提高他们的士气和生产力。 快乐的内部客户更好地服务最终客户。 任何值得一试的现场服务软件都提供一些基本的定制选项。 例如，用户可以调整字体或对列表视图进行排序。 这些选项改善了用户体验。 更好的用户体验会让现场代理更快乐并提高生产力。 技术赋能的现场服务企业允许现场用户根据自己的喜好调整移动应用程序界面。 每个用户都可以以最适合他们的方式设计他们的界面。 他们可以在数字模式下创建他们习惯的离线表格的副本，并在不受干扰的情况下继续他们的工作。 对场景的破坏和对模式的习惯是数字化现场服务的主要缺点。 基于配置文件的定制也迎合了内部客户的需求。 每组用户都会获得一个专用的应用程序，其中包含做好工作所需的全套资源。 现场代理获得未结工作订单列表和客户的 CRM 详细信息。

定制使企业具有弹性、灵活性和适应变化的能力。 强大的现场服务软件推动现场服务行业的定制化。 一套好的套件可确保合适的现场代理在合适的时间到达合适的客户。 它还使企业能够生成符合每个客户的需求和偏好的自定义工作流。 缺乏这样的基础设施可能会导致客户体验中断和缺失。

来自 15 年以上职业生涯的建议，适用于刚开始其旅程的新手和初级开发人员。

一旦暂停，reachout123 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，reachout123 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，reachout123 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 reachout123 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 reachout123：

降低 reachout123 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 reachout123 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:


The fragmented business landscape makes customization a necessity rather than a choice. Enterprises that stick on to rigid processes, and deliver one-size-fits-all services, become obsolete.

** The shift from Process Orientation to Customer Orientation**

Today’s business ecosystem moves at a fast pace. Customer sentiments and demands are fickle and shift fast. Businesses innovate to offer differentiated services and cope with such changes. Such differentiation often necessitates customized stacks. Inflexible business processes make field agents fall into a routine. They become accustomed to a rigid process and incapable of change. Large service companies design flexible business models, to make changes based on the demands of each customer. The overhaul their processes to focus on customer needs. Process efficiency becomes subservient to customer preferences.

A customer-focused system captures specific customer demands and preferences and generates a workflow. The work order captures the unique demands made by the customer. The work order syncs with the inventory management suite, to check for parts availability. An automated ordering system orders for any missing parts, and keeps the customer informed of availability. Once the parts and a competent technician fall in place, the system dispatches a field agent to do the work. The field agent app contains all relevant details, including the best route to reach the worksite, copy of work order, and service history. Field service agents may change the work order, based on the on-site situation.


Artificial Intelligence enabled scheduling and dispatching lends dynamism to these key processes. AI-powered algorithms factor in the special needs of the customer. It matches customer needs to workforce availability, and schedules effectively. When dispatching, the system generates the best routes in real-time. If a customer cancels or rescheduled the appointment, the system finds a new slot. The technician on the way to the job gets a replacement job.

Greater Focus on Preventive Maintenance

Maintenance is becoming increasingly preventive. More businesses prefer annual contracts, with routine inspections, to pre-empt downtime and disruption. Advances in IoT enable connecting sensors to equipment and get real-time alerts. Sensors alert the command center when the fuel runs low, or when vital operating parameters deviate outside the normal range. Such alerts enable field service personnel to make prompt, preventive maintenance. Pre-empting issues are much cheaper and less disruptive, compared to reactive fixes. Successful preventive maintenance needs a change in approach from the field service. The field service supporting the business has to become more customer-oriented. They have to tailor their services to suit the needs of different customers. Offering generic packages and expecting customers to adapt is untenable. The Need for Flexibility and Resilience Today’s customers demand fast service and transparency. They have a high level of awareness and expect the service representative to match their awareness. They also expect engagement on their terms, at the time of their choosing, and through the channel they choose. They go elsewhere if confronted with delay or poor service Field service businesses segment users to enable customization. Creating user profiles makes it easy to tailor the experience for each customer. For instance, a profile for premium customers, who pays for a superior experience, makes it easy to offer priority in dispatching. Enabling such profile-based customization requires a change in approach. Field services have to integrate customization as part of the normal workflow. Regarding customization as a premium add-on, or as a favor to the customer, will not work.

Today’s customers also change their minds frequently. At times, they demand changes due to changes in upstream or downstream workflow, or due to changing business conditions. Schedulers and dispatchers of large enterprises manage a large field workforce. These field agents, positioned at different places, have varying skills. Relying on human judgment and manual interventions to shuffle work assignments based on customer requests leads to mix-ups. Such mix-ups retard productivity and compromise field agent safety. It also degrades the customer experience. Processing such requests through the field service software makes customization seamless.

Delighting the Internal Customer

Many enterprises make serious mistakes in defining the customer. The external customer brings in revenue, and should always be the prime focus. But field services have another set of internal customers–the field agent or technicians. Field agents are the direct, and often the only interface between the end customer and the business. Satisfying the internal customers improves their morale and productivity. Happy internal customers serve end customers better. Any field service software worth its byte offers some basic customization options. For instance, users may adjust the font, or sort the list views. Such options improve the UX. Better UX leads to happier field agents and improved productivity. Tech endowed field service enterprises allow field users to tweak the mobile app interface to their liking. Each user may design their interface in the way most comfortable to them. They may create a replica of their accustomed-to offline forms in the digital mode, and continue their work without disruption. Disruption to the set and accustomed to patterns is a major drawback of digitizing field service. Profile-based customization also caters to the needs of internal customers. Each group of users gets a dedicated app, with the full set of resources needed to do their work well. The field agent gets a list of open work orders and the CRM details of the customers.

Customization makes the enterprise resilient, flexible, and adaptable to change. A robust field service software drives customization in the field service industry. A good suite ensures the right field agent reaches the right customer at the right time. It also enables the enterprise to generate custom workflows attuned to the needs and preferences of each customer . The lack of such an infrastructure risks a disconnected and missing experience for the customer.

Advice from a career of 15+ years for new and beginner developers just getting started on their journey.

Once unsuspended, reachout123 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

If reachout123 is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag reachout123:

Make all posts by reachout123 less visible

Report other inappropriate conduct

item.author: reachout123

item.title: 您留下的数据——攻击者的观点 | The Data You‘ve Left Behind – an Attacker‘s Perspective
item.description:
作为开发人员，我们看到大量数据在我们手中传递——根据网络上的多个来源（ Statista等），网络上的数据总量以数十泽字节为单位——一个泽字节等于一千拍字节（PB )，1 PB 等于 1000 太字节 (TB)。

一些数据是我们目前正在使用的数据，而一些数据相当于我们的旧项目——我们很可能重新处理、掩埋、从中吸取教训的项目，以及从未见过曙光的项目。 所有这些数据都非常重要——部分原因是我们已经学会了如何构建更强大的应用程序，但对于有兴趣窃取我们辛勤工作的一切的攻击者来说，这些数据也非常有价值。 在此博客中，我们将从攻击者的角度带您穿过数据的黑暗通道。

对攻击者来说重要的是什么？

无论您发现自己从事的是哪种项目，请始终牢记攻击者的目标是：

  • 与使用该产品的客户相关的数据——此类数据通常包括电子邮件地址、用户名和 IP 地址。 所有这些数据类对攻击者都至关重要，因为它们可用于发起身份盗用攻击。 身份盗窃攻击通常也会导致其他服务的数据泄露，并且循环重复。
  • 与产品本身相关的数据——此类数据包括与公司知识产权相关的数据：根据攻击者的目的，他们可能对产品的构建方式、使用的技术类型、是否产品所基于的网站正在运行过时的软件等。
  • 任何应该删除但没有删除的数据——此类数据可能包括来自存档、备份或其他可轻松在线访问的产品相关服务的数据（想想 CDN、不安全的备份存储位置等）

所有攻击者都对公司拥有的数据以及可以说“遗留下来”的数据感兴趣。 这就是为什么我们不仅要努力保护我们的应用程序，还要努力保护我们的数据库免受即将到来的威胁。

什么对我们很重要？

正如攻击者需要重点关注的事情一样，开发人员和数据分析师也应该关注一些事情。 我们应该关注的一些事情显然应该包括我们的应用程序和数据库的安全性，不要在数据库中存储不必要的数据，并且如果认为有必要，请注意使用 ISO 27001、HIPAA 或其他证书对应用程序进行认证，取决于产品所处的行业。

然而，随着时间的推移，我们可能会注意到有些事情比其他事情更重要——我们关注的最重要的事情之一就是数据库和其中的数据。

遗留在数据库中的数据

安全和隐私专家建议我们遵守 GDPR、ISO 27001 等安全法规是有充分理由的——此类安全法规对公司应如何处理数据和保护数据以及一些法规（尤其是 GDPR）提出了严格的要求对存储不必要的数据特别严格——我们无人看管的不必要数据越多，攻击者就越容易对我们的系统造成伤害。

不过，无需担心 - 了解一点有关应用程序安全性的知识并了解您使用 SQL 客户端的方式将大有帮助。 适当的 SQL 客户端具有以下功能来帮助我们处理数据：

  • 它们使我们可以轻松地将数据导入到我们选择的数据库中。
  • 它们中的大多数让我们观察与某个数据库相关的表、列、事件和触发器。 一些 SQL 客户端还让我们观察存储过程和做其他事情。 以下是使用DbVisualizer时的所有内容：


所有优秀的 SQL 客户端都会为我们提供查看特定于我们的表的数据类型的能力，有些甚至会让我们像电子表格一样编辑表中的数据。

不过，我们应该非常小心地处理数据——虽然一方面它对某些操作很有用，但我们可以放心，一旦发生数据泄露，留在数据库中的任何数据都可以重新用于针对另一个应用程序。 这就是为什么留下无人看管的数据为攻击者提供了另一个目的——公司拥有的此类数据越多，撞库攻击成功的可能性就越大。

凭据填充攻击通常是攻击者滥用从其他系统窃取的数据的直接结果——在这种攻击中，从一项服务窃取的凭据也用于访问另一项通常不相关的服务。

为避免此类攻击，请确保您正在使用的应用程序遵循严格的安全标准，并且如果您发现自己正在使用像 DbVisualizer 这样的 SQL 客户端，请留意它们为您提供其他帮助您识别可能指标的功能妥协或完全防止妥协发生。 这些东西可能包含以下一项或多项：

  • 是否添加、修改或删除了任何数据？ 当场可能妥协。 图 2 - DbVisualizer 提供的 SQL 历史表

  • 查看和更改与某些数据库、表或其列相关的权限的能力——因为权限是数据库安全的基石之一，确保仅将特定权限分配给特定用户甚至列很长的路要走。 在这种情况下，我们可以看到所有列都具有 SELECT、INSERT、UPDATE 和 REFERENCES 权限 - 请参阅您选择的数据库管理系统的文档，找出必要权限的用途，并相应地分配它们。 观察权限的能力是一种数据泄露预防措施：它帮助我们采用需要知道的原则，在攻击即将发生时抵御攻击。


  • 安全连接到数据库实例的能力——当我们不使用 SSH 连接到有问题的数据库时，我们可能会通过终端观察先前执行的命令列表，让每个人都可以看到我们的用户名和密码。 如果攻击者找到一种通过终端访问服务器的方法，但他看不到任何先前执行的命令，其中一些可能被认为是敏感的，那已经是胜利了。 通过 SSH 设置访问的选项是一种数据泄露预防措施。


  • 最后，请记住，DbVisualizer 还为其用户提供了为某些类型的查询配置权限的能力——DbVisualizer 可以允许我们仅在测试环境中运行某些查询，一些在生产环境中，一些在两者中，等等。DbVisualizer 带有一个几个可用选项：

    • 为任何环境（开发、测试或生产）配置“允许”选项可让查询漫游而不受干扰。
    • 配置“询问”选项将在执行之前询问用户是否执行有问题的 SQL 查询，并且
    • 配置“拒绝”选项将完全拒绝查询执行过程。 如果您发现自己在使用 DbVisualizer，请牢记此功能 - 这些选项对于确保我们的数据库实例的最高安全性是必不可少的，因为它们的配置是安全性“必须知道”原则的基础：人们应该只知道履行其在职职责所绝对需要的信息，但仅此而已。

还可以为应用程序的各个部分配置所有选项：一些权限在 SQL 命令的范围内工作，一些在表数据编辑器中工作，一些在过程编辑器中工作，一些只在连接到数据库有关。 它们中的每一个都允许我们保护我们数据库的一部分：权限选项可以用作数据泄露预防措施：但是，请注意该功能仅在 DbVisualizer 领域内可用。


如果某种类型的查询设置为“询问”或“确认”，DbVisualizer 将在定义的环境中要求确认：


结合这三个选项将帮助您抵御潜在的撞库攻击——当然，仅使用 SQL 客户端并不能解决问题，因此另外请确保您的 Web 应用程序遵循所有必要的 Web 安全标准（GDPR、ISO 27001，HIPAA（如果您工作的公司从事医疗保健业务等），您应该可以开始了。

我们希望您从这个博客中学到了一些有用的东西，并且您会回到DbVisualizer 的博客，直到下一次！

关于作者

他运营着世界上最大和最快的数据泄露搜索引擎之一 - BreachDirectory.com ，经常在多个地方的会议和博客上发表演讲，包括他在lukasvileikis.com上的博客。

当 Node.js 处理数据库查询等任务时，幕后会发生什么？ 我们将按照这段代码逐步探索它。
